title: Constitution of India - Article 14 (Equality before law)
summary: Article 14 guarantees equality before law and equal protection of laws.
text: Article 14 is commonly invoked in arbitrariness challenges against state action. In petition drafting, connect facts to unreasonable classification, unequal treatment, or lack of rational nexus.

title: Constitution of India - Article 21 (Life and personal liberty)
summary: Article 21 protects life and personal liberty except by procedure established by law.
text: Article 21 is used where procedure is unfair, disproportionate, or violates due process values. Drafting should clearly state direct impact on liberty and procedural defects.

title: CrPC Section 437 - Bail in non-bailable offences
summary: Section 437 governs bail powers of courts other than High Court and Sessions Court.
text: For bail workflows, evaluate gravity of offence, antecedents, possibility of tampering, and flight risk. The petition must include cooperation undertakings and local surety details.

title: CrPC Section 438 - Anticipatory bail
summary: Section 438 enables anticipatory bail by High Court or Court of Session.
text: Anticipatory bail drafts should address apprehension of arrest, clean antecedents, and willingness to cooperate. Mention jurisdiction-specific conditions and interim protection prayer.

title: IPC Section 420 - Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property
summary: Section 420 addresses cheating with dishonest inducement and property delivery.
text: For fraud complaints or defence, map intent at inception, inducement, and property transfer chronology. Distinguish civil breach from criminal mens rea in pleadings.

title: Limitation Act Section 5 - Extension of prescribed period
summary: Section 5 allows delay condonation on sufficient cause in specified proceedings.
text: Condonation applications should provide a day-by-day explanation where possible, supported by records. Avoid generic reasons and show bona fide conduct throughout.

title: Limitation Act Section 18 - Effect of acknowledgment
summary: Section 18 can reset limitation where acknowledgment is in writing before expiry.
text: When limitation is disputed, check for signed acknowledgment before the original period expires. Capture document date, signer capacity, and exact language.

title: CPC Order XXXIX - Temporary injunctions
summary: Order 39 governs temporary injunction and interlocutory relief.
text: Interim injunction drafting should plead prima facie case, balance of convenience, and irreparable injury. Add urgency, status quo facts, and supporting annexures.

title: Indian Evidence Act Section 65B - Electronic evidence
summary: Section 65B covers admissibility of electronic records with certificate requirements.
text: For digital evidence, identify device chain, record generation process, and certificate signatory. Pleadings should map each exhibit to authenticity and relevance.

title: Indian Kanoon - Search and citation guidance
summary: Practical guide for locating judgments, parallel citations, and court-level filtering.
text: Use court filters and phrase search to reduce false positives. Verify neutral citation, bench strength, and treatment history before relying on a case.

title: eCourts Services - Case status and filing references
summary: Official portal reference for case status tracking and filing information.
text: Use eCourts for filing stage, next hearing date, and causelist linkage where available. Align draft compliance checklists with current filing status.
